_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-10 SPRN Transport Destination (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required. Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-10: SPRN Transport Destination — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Establish destination hospital guidance for EMS transport of Special Pathogen (SPRN) patients, including authority for cross-MCA boundary transport and deviation from standard closest-hospital rules.

sections.title: SPRN Overview
content: SPRN: Special Pathogen Response Network — Michigan's coordinated system for managing patients with highly infectious diseases of public health concern
SPRN is ACTIVATED for: High Consequence Infectious Diseases (HCIDs) including Ebola (Ebola Virus Disease/EVD), Marburg, Lassa, and other diseases designated by MDHHS or CDC
MCA OPTIONAL: All SPRN protocols (10-10 through 10-18) apply ONLY in MCAs and EMS agencies that have been selected for and activated in the SPRN program
Non-SPRN agencies: do NOT transport SPRN patients — contact online MC for instructions; stage and await SPRN team if needed

sections.title: Destination Hospital — SPRN Transport
content: SPRN transport destination = CLOSEST APPROPRIATE hospital with SPRN capability — NOT necessarily the closest hospital
Appropriate SPRN hospital: facility with confirmed SPRN/HCID isolation capacity and trained clinical team
SPRN destination selection: made by MDHHS and SPRN program coordinator in consultation with online medical control
EMS transport team does NOT independently select hospital for SPRN patients — receive destination from MDHHS/SPRN coordinator
If patient is critical and SPRN hospital is not immediately accessible: transport to closest ED; notify ED and SPRN coordinator en route

sections.title: Cross-MCA Boundary Transport
content: SPRN transport may occur ACROSS MCA boundaries — this is explicitly authorized under SPRN protocols
Cross-MCA SPRN transport: responding agency operates under their home MCA protocols (consistent with Protocol 10-7)
Destination hospital may be in a different MCA, region, or county — follow SPRN coordinator direction regardless of typical service area boundaries
Notify both home MCA and destination MCA of cross-boundary SPRN transport
Inter-facility SPRN transport (hospital to hospital) is also authorized under SPRN — inter-facility team may cross MCA lines to move patient to higher SPRN capability facility

sections.title: Notification Requirements
content: Prior to transport: notify receiving hospital of patient type, SPRN activation, ETA, and patient status
Receiving hospital must confirm readiness (SPRN isolation room, receiving team in PPE) before EMS arrives
Notify home MCA medical director of SPRN transport
Notify MDHHS SPRN coordinator — they will coordinate hospital readiness
Do NOT transport without confirmed hospital receiving team in place

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols (10-10 through 10-18) are only active for EMS agencies designated and activated within Michigan's Special Pathogen Response Network (SPRN). Non-SPRN agencies do not use these protocols.

deterioration_flags: SPRN patient deteriorates during transport — life support decisions per Protocol 10-14; contact SPRN coordinator and online MC immediately
Receiving hospital unable to accept SPRN patient — contact MDHHS coordinator for alternate destination; do NOT divert independently
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18 apply only to SPRN-designated agencies. Transport destination is determined by MDHHS/SPRN coordinator, not standard closest-hospital rules. Cross-MCA SPRN transport is explicitly authorized.
